## 1.4.1

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### Consistent document layout widths

Enforce consistent layout widths by using CSS `ch` instead of ratio widths. (fixes #59)

This ensures consistency in the word wrapping and line breaks between (1) how you see it in Obsidian, and (2) your final rendered PDF.

Of course, that's assuming you don't meddle too much with CSS in Obsidian, as well as standard Fountain-to-PDF settings. This is accomplished by using the `ch` width unit, which ensures consistent width since screenplays use a monospace font. You can be confident in how the

Minor breaking change: if you were previously using the old CSS tokens for widths (e.g. `--fountain--character-left-factor`), they are removed, and instead simplified to just 4 tokens:

```css
// Current default tokens:
--fountain-line-width: 60ch;
--fountain-character-line-width: 16ch;
--fountain-dialogue-line-width: 38ch;
--fountain-parenthetical-line-width: 29ch;
```

This sleeker, cleaner method, unfortunately, becomes funky-looking when on the most popular Obsidian theme. Minimal provides some really cool line-width utilities, which is why I personally use it, but it breaks this.

There is now a toggle in Settings to fix the styling if you are using Minimal theme.

### Markdown Blockquote Annotations

You can now use Markdown blockquotes as screenplay annotations. This is a new more stable alternative to the `%% Obsidian comment %%` annotation -- see [#58](https://github.com/chuangcaleb/obsidian-fountain-editor/pull/58/files). There is also now a setting to opt-out of formatting Fountain forced-Transitions.

Note that if you use Markdown blockquotes, you will need to strip your document of blockquotes before rendering Fountain to PDF.

- 1a75126: Skip processing Obsidian multi-line blockquotes as Fountain forced Transitions
- a298bc0: Add option to opt-out of formatting Fountain forced-Transitions

### Technical

- 0ce2783: dx: manage versioning with changesets

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description: ## What's Changed ### Features - Fountain notes detected by the plugin will now have a `fountain` class on the .markdown-source-view element - Users can target styling with this - Fountain notes will not parse inside Obsidian comments, allowing them to be escape hatches to markdown! - they now have a more significant custom color ### Fixes - Styling - centered text is more center aligned - dialogue margins larger on right side - some css refactored, take note ### Perf - Improved Fountain detection into CodeMirror StateField, should be less checks **Full Changelog**: https://github.com/chuangcaleb/obsidian-fountain-editor/compare/1.2.0...1.3.0

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## What's Changed

User:
* 1.2.0 by @chuangcaleb in https://github.com/chuangcaleb/obsidian-fountain-editor/pull/48
	- allow Obsidian %% comment blocks as escape hatches to Obsidian markdown!
	- accept .fountain.md and #fountain tags to mark a file as Fountain
	- tweaked css selector

Dev:
- fix: broken link in README to CONTRIBUTING by @wesleyboar in https://github.com/chuangcaleb/obsidian-fountain-editor/pull/29
	- @wesleyboar made their first contribution in https://github.com/chuangcaleb/obsidian-fountain-editor/pull/29
- update deps
- introduce xo as code linter/formatter

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/chuangcaleb/obsidian-fountain-editor/compare/1.1.4...1.2.0

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Ah. So styling breaks on Minimal theme, which does line-width styling in a totally different way. So let's remove fountain-left and fountain-center for now, and use min(100%, ... ) for mobile styling! 

Oh and do read [the previous release changelog](https://github.com/chuangcaleb/obsidian-fountain-editor/releases/tag/1.1.0) about the public launch of this plugin into the community catalog! :DD

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/chuangcaleb/obsidian-fountain-editor/compare/1.0.3...1.1.1

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Wow! We're finally [community-plugin approved](https://github.com/obsidianmd/obsidian-releases/pull/2519)! Special thanks goes out to y'all early supporters. Everyone who's given this project its first 10 stars, and Thomsen@Discord who's given great dialogue.

The major update is that we now support Fountain styling on mobile! This is achieved using calculated ratio-percentages for Fountain's token indentation.

Besides that, there's some docs and dx changes.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/chuangcaleb/obsidian-fountain-editor/compare/1.0.3...1.1.0

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
This release is a lot of cleaning and optimization; namely, where files without the `cssclasses:fountain` frontmatter won't continually parse for Fountain syntax, which is great.

DX stuff also includes rollup + postcss.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/chuangcaleb/obsidian-fountain-editor/compare/1.0.2...1.0.3
